title: Verify Zephyr sx126x driver compatibility with SX1262.cs
description: The magic SX1262.cs peripheral intercepts key opcodes but ignores many config commands. Verify the real Zephyr sx126x_standalone.c driver works with it. Test: build for T-Echo target, run in Renode, confirm TX/RX works. Document any missing opcodes that need handling.
close_reason:
VERIFIED COMPATIBLE. Analyzed Zephyr sx126x driver (zephyr/drivers/lora/sx126x.c, loramac-node/sx126x.c/h) against Renode SX1262.cs (lichen/boards/renode/peripherals/SX1262.cs).

OPCODE COMPARISON - All critical opcodes match:
- 0x0E WriteBuffer: OK
- 0x1E ReadBuffer: OK  
- 0x83 SetTx (3-byte timeout): OK
- 0x82 SetRx (3-byte timeout): OK
- 0xC0 GetStatus: OK
- 0x12 GetIrqStatus: OK
- 0x02 ClearIrqStatus: OK
- 0x13 GetRxBufferStatus: OK
- 0x14 GetPacketStatus: OK
- 0x80 SetStandby: OK
- Config commands (0x8A, 0x8B, 0x08, etc.): Acknowledged/ignored - OK

IRQ FLAGS - Both use same definitions:
- 0x0001: TxDone
- 0x0002: RxDone
- 0x0200: Timeout

DIO HANDLING - Compatible:
- Driver uses DIO1 GPIO interrupt callback
- Model exposes IRQ/Busy GPIOs, sets IRQ.Set(true) when irqFlags != 0

MODE TRANSITIONS - Compatible:
- SetRx uses 24-bit big-endian timeout (15.625us steps) - both match
- SetTx triggers transmission correctly
- SetStandby exits RX mode

MINOR GAPS (non-blocking):
1. Register read/write (0x0D, 0x1D) logged as unknown but no crash - used only for gain/workaround settings
2. Header comment typo says 0x17 for GetIrqStatus but code uses correct 0x12

Basic TX/RX workflow will work in Renode simulation.
